 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.2","title":"Implement execute CLI commands","description":"## Task
Implement CLI commands for executing approved requests.

## Commands

### slb execute <request-id>
- Flags: --session-id/-s
- Flags: --timeout (seconds, default 300)
- Flags: --background (don't wait, return PID)
- Validates gate conditions
- Executes in caller's environment
- JSON: { exit_code, duration_ms, log_path }

### slb emergency-execute "<command>"
- Human override that bypasses approval
- Flags: --reason (required)
- Flags: --capture-rollback
- Flags: --yes (skip interactive confirmation)
- Flags: --ack "<sha256(command)>" (required with --yes)
- Requires interactive confirmation OR --yes --ack
- Logs extensively for audit
- JSON: { exit_code, logged_at, reason }

### slb rollback <request-id>
- Requires rollback data was captured
- Restores captured state
- JSON: { request_id, rolled_back_at }

## Error Cases
- Not approved: "Request is not in approved state"
- Approval expired: "Approval has expired, please re-request"
- Hash mismatch: "Command was modified since approval"
- Already executed: "Request has already been executed"

## Files
- internal/cli/execute.go
- internal/cli/emergency.go
- internal/cli/rollback.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Execute validates all gate conditions
- [ ] Command runs in caller's environment
- [ ] Emergency requires confirmation or --yes --ack
- [ ] Rollback restores captured state
- [ ] All output is JSON compatible","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueBear","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:20:11.282654838-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:15:41.491540745-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:15:41.491545985-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:20:11.283601211-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v0z.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:20:11.284485618-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.3","title":"Implement dry-run pre-flight for supported commands","description":"## Task
Implement dry-run pre-flight checks for commands that support it.

## Supported Commands (from plan)

### kubectl
- Original: kubectl delete deployment foo
- Dry-run: kubectl delete deployment foo --dry-run=client
- Output: Shows what would be deleted

### terraform
- Original: terraform destroy
- Dry-run: terraform plan -destroy
- Output: Shows resources that would be destroyed

### rm
- Original: rm -rf ./build
- Dry-run: ls -la ./build (show what would be removed)
- Output: File listing with sizes

### git reset
- Original: git reset --hard HEAD~5
- Dry-run: git diff HEAD~5..HEAD
- Output: Shows commits that would be lost

### helm
- Original: helm uninstall myrelease
- Dry-run: helm get manifest myrelease
- Output: Shows resources that would be removed

## Functions (internal/core/dryrun.go)

### RunDryRun(spec *CommandSpec) (*DryRunResult, error)
1. Detect command type from spec
2. Generate dry-run variant
3. Execute dry-run command
4. Capture and format output
5. Return result

### GetDryRunCommand(cmd string) (string, bool)
- Return dry-run variant if supported
- Return false if no dry-run available

## Files
- internal/core/dryrun.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] kubectl --dry-run works
- [ ] terraform plan -destroy works
- [ ] rm shows file listing
- [ ] git shows diff
- [ ] Graceful fallback for unsupported","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownLake","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:25:05.04879799-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:29:30.424921253-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:27:37.927951738-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.3","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:25:05.049776051-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.4","title":"Implement rollback state capture","description":"## Task
Implement optional rollback state capture before destructive operations.

## What to Capture

### File System (rm)
- Create tarball of affected paths
- Store in .slb/rollback/req-<id>.tar.gz
- Include metadata (permissions, timestamps)

### Git
- Store current HEAD commit hash
- Capture stash of uncommitted changes
- Store branch name

### Kubernetes
- kubectl get <resource> -o yaml before delete
- Store in .slb/rollback/req-<id>.yaml

### Database (if applicable)
- pg_dump / mysqldump of affected tables
- Store schema + data

## Functions (internal/core/rollback.go)

### CaptureRollbackState(req *Request) (*RollbackData, error)
1. Detect command type
2. Execute capture strategy
3. Store data in .slb/rollback/
4. Return metadata

### RestoreRollbackState(data *RollbackData) error
1. Validate rollback data exists
2. Execute restore strategy
3. Verify restoration
4. Log outcome

## Configuration
[general]
enable_rollback_capture = true

## Storage
- .slb/rollback/req-<uuid>/
  - metadata.json
  - files.tar.gz
  - manifest.yaml
  - etc.

## Files
- internal/core/rollback.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File capture creates valid tarball
- [ ] Git state captured correctly
- [ ] Kubernetes manifests captured
- [ ] Restore works
- [ ] Storage cleaned up after retention","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCastle","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:25:19.447633852-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:47:52.561068473-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:47:52.561068473-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.4","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:25:19.448557502-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kt1","title":"Phase 4: CLI Testing Documentation & Patterns Guide","description":"## Purpose & Rationale

Documentation is the force multiplier that makes all other phases sustainable. This phase captures:
1. Testing patterns discovered during implementation
2. Decision rationale for what we tested vs. accepted at 0%
3. Guidelines for future CLI command tests

Without documentation, future maintainers will:
- Reinvent patterns we already figured out
- Question why some functions are at 0% (intentional vs. oversight)
- Write inconsistent tests

## Deliverables

### 1. CLI Testing Patterns Guide
Location: internal/cli/TESTING.md

Contents:
- Test file structure conventions
- Cobra command testing with executeCommand helper
- Database fixture patterns with testutil
- JSON vs text output testing
- Flag parsing edge cases
- Coverage exclusion rationale

### 2. Coverage Acceptance Matrix
Document which functions are intentionally at <100% and why:

| Function | Coverage | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| Execute | 0% | Main entry, os.Exit |
| followFile | 0% | Signal handling, tested via integration |
| ... | ... | ... |

### 3. Test Pattern Examples
One comprehensive example test per pattern type:
- Command structure test
- Flag parsing test
- Database integration test
- Error path test
- Output format test

## Why This Is Phase 4 (Last)

Documentation should reflect actual implementation, not aspirations:
1. We need to actually implement patterns before documenting them
2. Decisions may change during implementation
3. Edge cases discovered in Phase 1-3 inform the guide

## Success Criteria
- TESTING.md exists and is comprehensive
- Coverage acceptance matrix is complete
- At least 5 example patterns documented
- New contributor can understand CLI testing in <30 minutes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:26:15.951508622-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T10:27:17.945536781-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kt1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-59b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:27:29.496936844-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-l0f","title":"Unit Tests: Utils Package (0% → 90% coverage)","description":"## Background and Purpose

The internal/utils package provides shared utility functions used across the codebase. Currently at 0% test coverage, this package contains foundational utilities that other packages depend on, making thorough testing essential for overall system reliability.

## Current State

The utils package likely includes:
- strings.go: String manipulation utilities
- time.go: Time formatting and parsing
- hash.go: Command hashing utilities
- env.go: Environment variable helpers
- paths.go: Path manipulation

Key utility functions:
- Command normalization and hashing
- Timestamp formatting/parsing
- Environment variable resolution
- Path canonicalization
- String truncation and formatting

## Why Utils Testing is Critical

1. **Foundation**: Other packages depend on these utilities
2. **Edge Cases**: Utility functions often have subtle edge cases
3. **Regression Prevention**: Small changes can have wide impact
4. **Documentation**: Tests serve as usage examples

## Testing Strategy

### Test Categories

1. **String Utility Tests**
   - Empty string handling
   - Unicode support
   - Truncation edge cases
   - Case conversion

2. **Time Utility Tests**
   - RFC3339 parsing
   - Duration formatting
   - Timezone handling
   - Relative time (e.g., '5m ago')

3. **Hash Utility Tests**
   - Command normalization
   - Hash stability (same input = same output)
   - Collision resistance
   - Unicode handling

4. **Path Utility Tests**
   - Absolute vs relative paths
   - Symlink resolution
   - Home directory expansion
   - Cross-platform paths

5. **Environment Tests**
   - Variable expansion
   - Default values
   - Required vs optional
   - Type coercion

### Test File Structure
- utils/strings_test.go
- utils/time_test.go  
- utils/hash_test.go
- utils/env_test.go
- utils/paths_test.go

## Dependencies
- DEPENDS ON: [Test Infrastructure bead] - minimal, mostly standalone

## Success Criteria
- go test ./internal/utils/... shows 90%+ coverage
- All edge cases covered
- Unicode handling verified
- No panics on nil/empty input

## Implementation Notes

Table-driven tests work well for utilities:
  tests := []struct{
      name string
      input string
      want string
  }{
      {'empty', '', ''},
      {'simple', 'hello', 'HELLO'},
      {'unicode', 'héllo', 'HÉLLO'},
  }

Focus on boundary conditions and error cases.

## Estimated Effort: Small","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownLake","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:00:18.714998767-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T21:45:57.026478166-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T21:45:57.026478166-06:00","labels":["priority-medium","testing","utils"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-l0f","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v2x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:00:18.718149937-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8","title":"EPIC: Pattern Matching & Risk Classification","description":"## Overview
Implement the three-tier risk classification system that determines which commands need approval and at what level.

## Background & Rationale
Not all dangerous commands are equally dangerous. The plan defines three tiers:
- CRITICAL: Catastrophic, irreversible (2+ approvals, human notification)
- DANGEROUS: Significant, recoverable with effort (1 approval)
- CAUTION: Annoying if wrong, easily fixed (auto-approved after 30s)

Plus a SAFE tier for commands that skip review entirely.

## Key Features (from plan)
1. **Pattern Matching**: Regex patterns per tier, case-insensitive
2. **Command Normalization**: Strip sudo/env wrappers, detect compound commands
3. **Path Resolution**: Expand relative paths to absolute for safety checks
4. **Pattern Precedence**: SAFE → CRITICAL → DANGEROUS → CAUTION
5. **Agent Pattern Management**: Agents can ADD patterns (making things safer) but CANNOT remove
6. **Dynamic Quorum**: If fewer agents available, adjust required approvals

## Key Commands
- `slb patterns list [--tier <tier>]` → list patterns
- `slb patterns test "<command>"` / `slb check "<command>"` → show tier
- `slb patterns add --tier <tier> "<pattern>" --reason "..."` → agents can add
- `slb patterns request-removal "<pattern>"` → creates pending removal request
- `slb patterns suggest --tier <tier> "<pattern>"` → propose for human review

## Files
- internal/core/patterns.go (matching logic)
- internal/config/patterns.go (default patterns)
- internal/cli/patterns.go
- internal/db/pattern_changes.go, custom_patterns.go

## Default Pattern Categories
- File System: rm -rf, chmod -R, chown -R
- Git: reset --hard, clean -fd, push --force
- Kubernetes: delete node/namespace/pv/pvc
- Databases: DROP DATABASE/SCHEMA/TABLE, DELETE without WHERE
- Cloud: terraform destroy, aws terminate-instances
- Containers: docker system prune -a

## Success Criteria
1. Commands correctly classified into tiers
2. Normalization handles sudo, env, compound commands
3. Agents can add but not remove patterns
4. Pattern test returns JSON with needs_approval, tier, min_approvals","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:11:57.291380377-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:11:57.291380377-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:11:57.29266506-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.1","title":"Implement pattern matching engine with command normalization","description":"## Task
Implement the pattern matching engine that classifies commands into risk tiers.

## Core Functions (internal/core/patterns.go)

### ClassifyCommand(cmd string, cwd string) (RiskTier, *MatchResult)
- Normalize command
- Check tiers in order: SAFE → CRITICAL → DANGEROUS → CAUTION
- Return tier and match details

### NormalizeCommand(cmd string) (*NormalizedCommand, error)
- Parse with shell-aware tokenizer
- Extract primary command from wrappers: sudo, doas, env, command, builtin, time, nice, ionice, nohup
- Detect compound commands (;, &&, ||, |, subshells)
- If any segment matches tier, whole command is at least that tier
- If parsing fails, fall back to raw regex and upgrade tier by one step

### MatchPattern(pattern, cmd string) bool
- Regex matching, case-insensitive by default
- Handle special characters

### ResolvePathsInCommand(cmd, cwd string) string
- Expand relative paths (./,../) to absolute
- Used for path-aware patterns

## Pattern Precedence
1. Check SAFE patterns → skip review entirely
2. Check CRITICAL → highest risk
3. Check DANGEROUS
4. Check CAUTION
5. No match → allowed without review

## Match Result
- Tier
- MatchedPattern
- MinApprovals
- NeedsApproval (bool)

## Files
- internal/core/patterns.go
- internal/core/normalize.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Commands correctly classified
- [ ] sudo/env wrappers stripped
- [ ] Compound commands detected
- [ ] Paths resolved correctly
- [ ] Case-insensitive matching","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:17:09.132220861-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T19:39:45.690271534-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T19:39:45.690271534-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f5d.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:31:34.904807286-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
